title: Property Tests, Algebraic Laws, and Pipeline Diagnostics · Mastery Check
summary: Prove a normalization and aggregation library across generated changed cases and injected faults. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a law-oriented regression and diagnostics suite. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate concrete examples from general properties and generate boundary-rich cases.
Test identity, associativity, idempotence, or round-trip laws only where contracts claim them.
Attach diagnostic taps at effect boundaries without changing transformed values.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-functional immutable transformation portfolio."""

from collections.abc import Callable, Iterable, Iterator
from functools import partial, reduce
from itertools import chain, islice
from typing import TypeVar

T = TypeVar('T')
U = TypeVar('U')

# Extend one pure, lazy, tested pipeline increment per activity.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Example Properties value-and-effect pipeline for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Example Properties assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: A local decision core receives an injected clock and storage callable. Replaying the same 5-event fixture must reproduce decisions while I/O failures stay at the imperative boundary.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Example Properties has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.
